title: Analyst compression-mode: formalize operator-commissioned content-compression as a recurring analyst capability with mode='compression' EXP shape, HNOTE trigger, and decider HOLD-for-operator-review integration path
depends_on: PROP-043 (analyst-operator-commission-priority) — provides Mode 0.5 dispatcher tier that routes compression HNOTEs. PROP-042 plugs in as commission_type='compression'.
related_directives: DIRECTIVE-20260516-005 (PROP-041-amendment-002-categories-FGH — established that mechanical rewriter categories A-H cannot REDUCE content volume; compression-mode addresses that gap)
DIRECTIVE-20260516-006 (PROP-043 dispatcher fix that this PROP depends on)
DIRECTIVE-20260516-007 (amendment-002 approval; established the operator-direct compression precedent that this PROP formalizes)

headline_recommendation.selected_option: Compression-mode is OPERATOR-COMMISSIONED ONLY, triggered via HNOTE with commission=true + commission_type='compression', authored by analyst as EXP-NNN.json with mode='compression', integrated by decider via HOLD-for-operator-review (decider does NOT auto-route to curmudgeon). Mode-5-style discipline: never on cadence, never analyst-self-triggered. Dispatcher placement: Option C — reuse PROP-043's Mode 0.5 commission_type='compression' delegation; do NOT create a new Mode 6.
headline_recommendation.rationale_one_paragraph: EXP-401 demonstrated that content compression (not just mechanical reformatting) is viable for over-target WIN surfaces — WIN-012.detail_evidence went from 1888w → 528w (3.58x) with verdict, claim, all load-bearing numbers, and the 3-step refutation chain preserved (operator confirmed 'much better'). The work shape is recognizably different from mechanical rewriter Categories A-H (which can RESTRUCTURE but not REDUCE) and from normal Mode 1 deep-attack expansions (which ADD content rather than CUT). It deserves a formal mode-spec so the work pattern can recur without each invocation requiring a cowork-claude session. Dispatcher placement piggybacks on PROP-043's Mode 0.5 commission infrastructure — Option C avoids dispatcher complexity, lets PROP-042 stay tightly scoped to HNOTE-schema + EXP-shape + decider-intake specification, and avoids the Mode 5 overload that Option B would create. HOLD-for-operator-review is non-negotiable at launch (operator preference, matches EXP-401 pattern) — auto-route to curmudgeon is a future relaxation contingent on track record.
headline_recommendation.scoped_to: monitor/prompts/analyst.md (new compression-commission sub-procedure under Mode 0.5 delegation), monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md (extend EXP schema docs with mode='compression' fields), monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md (Step 2a extension for HOLD-for-operator-review handling), CLAUDE.md (compression-commission discipline rule). No changes to sloppytoppy-rewrite, no changes to existing Mode 0/1/1b/2/2b/3/4/5 procedures.

executive_summary.problem_one_line: The pipeline has 30+ over-target WIN surfaces that the mechanical rewriter (PROP-041) explicitly cannot fix — Categories A-H restructure prose but do not reduce content volume. The first Phase 2 rewriter run (2026-05-16) produced 1 RW and 4 PUNTs, 3 of which were length-dominated WINs the rewriter correctly deferred. EXP-401 showed that human-grade compression with preservation discipline can clear those surfaces (3.58x reduction, operator-approved), but the work currently lacks any pipeline home — it ran ad-hoc via cowork-claude in operator-attended session because the HNOTE that commissioned it sat behind Mode 1's tracker backlog.
executive_summary.evidence_one_line: EXP-401 (2026-05-16, integrated commit 66c9a74): WIN-012.detail_evidence 1888w → 528w; verdict 'Self-Contradicted' preserved, claim verbatim preserved, 10/15 load-bearing numbers preserved (5 cut belong to follow-up ISS), 3/3 key citations preserved, 5/5 cross-WIN refs preserved, test suite 3633 passed / 0 failed, operator confirmed 'much better' on side-by-side comparison.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Add a compression-mode commission_type to the analyst dispatcher (via PROP-043's Mode 0.5 delegation), define the EXP shape with mode='compression' and content-preservation-audit fields, extend decider Step 2a to recognize mode='compression' and route to HOLD-for-operator-review instead of auto-pushing to the curmudgeon queue.
executive_summary.shape_one_line: ~35 lines added to analyst.md (Mode 0.5 compression-commission sub-procedure block) + ~25 lines added to DATA-SCHEMAS.md (mode='compression' EXP schema definition) + ~30 lines added to decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md (Step 2a.4 compression HOLD-for-operator-review branch + Step 2a.5 operator-approval integration) + ~8 lines in CLAUDE.md (discipline rule). No new files.

C_Q1_dedicated_mode_field_vs_body_detect.answer: Dedicated `commission_type: 'compression'` field on the HNOTE, set explicitly by operator. Do NOT rely on body-content detection.
C_Q1_dedicated_mode_field_vs_body_detect.rationale: Body detection (Mode 5's pattern: `/frozen[- ]prediction/i.test(...)`) is acceptable for a single commission type whose name is unambiguous, but it doesn't scale to multiple commission types and is fragile under HNOTE-body drift. Explicit `commission_type` is self-documenting, easy to grep, easy to audit via tinker telemetry, and aligns with PROP-043's design. Mode 5's existing body-detection stays in place for backward-compat (frozen-pred HNOTEs filed before PROP-043/042 don't need the flag), but new commission types should be explicit-flag-only from the start.
C_Q1_dedicated_mode_field_vs_body_detect.alignment_with_PROP_043: PROP-043 specifies `commission_type` as the routing field (allowed values: 'frozen-prediction', 'compression', 'custom-rewrite', 'other'). PROP-042 adds 'compression' as a concrete commission_type with its own EXP shape and decider intake path. PROP-043's Mode 0.5 dispatcher block already says: 'If `commission_type` is `'compression'`, see PROP-042 procedure (TBD — landing in a separate amendment).' PROP-042 IS that procedure.

C_Q2_minimum_required_HNOTE_schema.answer: Required fields + optional/recommended fields enumerated below. The minimum is enforced at analyst-dispatcher pickup time (analyst rejects malformed compression HNOTEs back to operator with a missing-field list).

required_fields.status: must be 'pending' at file time; analyst flips to 'consumed' or 'consumed-pending-verification' per PROP-014 Mech 1b WRITE-VERIFY
required_fields.target_win_id_or_section_id: exactly ONE of: target_win_id (e.g., '012'), target_section_id (e.g., 'SEC-NNN'). Compression operates on one surface per HNOTE (see C-Q3)
required_fields.target_field: the specific field to compress, e.g., 'detail_evidence', 'detail_verdict_text', 'section_body' — must be a known field on the target_win_id's wins.json record or target_section_id's sections.json record
required_fields.target_word_count: integer — the operator's word-count target (the analyst aims at this; not a hard cap unless hard_cap_word_count is also set)
required_fields.hold_for_operator_review: true (boolean) — non-negotiable at launch per operator preference; signals decider to NOT auto-route to curmudgeon
required_fields.subject: short human-readable description (≤200 chars)
required_fields.body: longform operator instructions: what to preserve, what to cut, where to link out, etc.

subfields.verdict_must_remain: string or null — the WIN's verdict that compression must preserve (e.g., 'Self-Contradicted'). Null when target_field is not verdict-bearing.
subfields.claim_must_remain_verbatim: string or null — the WIN's claim text that compression must preserve VERBATIM (numbers, units, exact phrasing). Null when target_field is not claim-bearing.
subfields.load_bearing_numbers: array of strings — numerical claims (with units) that must remain in the compressed text
subfields.load_bearing_citations: array of strings — author-year-or-DOI citations that must remain (e.g., 'Van Camp et al. 2001 (10.1023/A:1013940928637)')
subfields.cross_win_refs_must_remain: array of WIN-NNN ids that must remain as hyperlinks
subfields.structural_section_refs_must_remain: array of section anchor ids that must remain as hyperlinks

strongly_recommended_fields.allowed_cuts: array of strings — content categories the operator authorizes the analyst to cut entirely (e.g., 'Chebychev filter defender-rescue paragraph', 'monitor.py tautology paragraph')
strongly_recommended_fields.allowed_link_out_destinations: array of strings — destinations where cut content can be linked rather than reproduced (e.g., 'WIN-011', 'Part 6.14 κ-cluster section')
strongly_recommended_fields.hard_cap_word_count: integer or null — absolute upper bound the analyst must not exceed; if null, target_word_count is soft and analyst can flag a higher count in EXP rationale if compression isn't viable
strongly_recommended_fields.follow_up_iss_allowed: boolean (default true) — whether analyst may propose follow-up ISS for cut content that's structurally separate and deserves its own surface (per EXP-401's GRACE-forensics example)

optional_fields.priority: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' — within-Mode 0.5 ordering tie-break if multiple compression HNOTEs pending. Default 'medium'.
optional_fields.deadline_hint: ISO timestamp — operator's preferred completion-by; non-binding hint
optional_fields.relates_to: array of related artifact IDs (prior HNOTEs, PROPs, EXPs, ISSs)
optional_fields.context_hints: free-form object with operator-side context the analyst may find useful

allowed_cuts: Yang & Wang reanalysis details (move to WIN-011 or cut)
Detailed walkthroughs of dome's live-site WIN-013 entry
example_HNOTE_matching_EXP_401_actual_st.subject: EXPERIMENT: Compress WIN-012.detail_evidence from 1888w to ~600w. Real content compression, not mechanical reformatting.
example_HNOTE_matching_EXP_401_actual_st.body: <longform operator instructions per EXP-401's actual HNOTE body — see monitor/analyst/human-notes.json>

C_Q2_minimum_required_HNOTE_schema.schema_enforcement: Analyst dispatcher reads the compression HNOTE on pickup. If any required field is missing, analyst writes a 'malformed compression HNOTE' note to monitor/decisions/human-notes.json (operator-facing) listing the missing fields and leaves the HNOTE status='pending-blocked-on-operator'. No auto-fix; operator amends the HNOTE.

C_Q3_one_surface_per_HNOTE_or_batch.answer: ONE surface per HNOTE. Multi-surface compression batches are filed as multiple HNOTEs.
C_Q3_one_surface_per_HNOTE_or_batch.rationale: Three reasons. (1) Invariant simplicity: one HNOTE → one EXP → one HOLD-for-operator-review → one wins.json/sections.json edit. Easier to track in lifecycle and easier to revert if one surface's compression fails operator review. (2) Operator-review surface area: compression EXPs require careful operator side-by-side review (per EXP-401 pattern); bundling 5 surfaces into one EXP creates a 5-decision review burden that doesn't scale and risks fatigue-driven approval. (3) Mode 0.5 FIFO: PROP-043's Mode 0.5 processes one commission per run. Bundling N surfaces into one HNOTE doesn't speed throughput (still one analyst-run per HNOTE); spreading N surfaces across N HNOTEs lets the operator decide whether all N are commission-priority or some are routine.
C_Q3_one_surface_per_HNOTE_or_batch.implication: Operator filing 'compress these 5 over-target WINs' creates 5 HNOTEs in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json (or one parent + 5 child commissions if a future amendment introduces that pattern; not in PROP-042 scope). Analyst processes them FIFO via PROP-043 Mode 0.5 over 5 successive runs.
C_Q3_one_surface_per_HNOTE_or_batch.future_amendment_path: If operator-experienced friction with N-batches in practice (>3 compression HNOTEs filed in one sitting), PROP-042-amendment-001 can add a parent-batch HNOTE schema. Defer until evidence of need.

C_Q4_cross_agent_compression_commissions.answer: ANALYST-EXCLUSIVE. Compression HNOTEs go only in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json. Curmudgeon-side and decider-side HNOTEs do NOT commission compression.

rationale.curmudgeon_side: Curmudgeon's HNOTEs are review-rubric tweaks, defender-pivot signals, and quality-gate adjustments. Curmudgeon does NOT author EXP files (it produces reviews); commissioning content compression via curmudgeon's HNOTE channel would require curmudgeon to either (a) commission analyst to do the compression (which is what an analyst-side HNOTE already does), or (b) produce compressed prose itself (out of curmudgeon's role). Either way, the channel of record is monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.
rationale.decider_side: Decider's HNOTEs are admin/configuration toggles (set_curmudgeon_mode, burndown approve/cancel, operator-routed queue pushes via universal-pusher). Decider does NOT author EXP files. Compression commission requires authoring; analyst is the author. Same conclusion: channel of record is monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.
rationale.future_caveat: If a future curmudgeon-side rubric discovery surfaces 'this WIN's verbosity is causing review-fatigue artifacts and would benefit from compression', curmudgeon's correct action is to FILE AN ISS-NNN routed to operator-review-suggest (not to commission compression directly). Operator decides whether to file an analyst-side compression HNOTE in response. This preserves the channel-of-record discipline.

C_Q4_cross_agent_compression_commissions.discipline_rule: Add to CLAUDE.md: 'Compression-commission HNOTEs are analyst-exclusive — file them in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json. Curmudgeon-side and decider-side HNOTEs are not channels for compression commissions; they're for rubric/admin work specific to those agents.'

D_Q1_HOLD_queue_vs_parallel_preview.answer: HOLD queue (no parallel curmudgeon preview at launch). Decider writes the compression EXP to a HOLD list in monitor/decisions/, does NOT push to curmudgeon priority queue, surfaces it for operator review in latest-decider-summary.txt, and waits for operator approval signal before integration.
D_Q1_HOLD_queue_vs_parallel_preview.rationale: Operator's stated preference is HOLD-for-operator-review (per directive's operator_preferences_for_proposal[2]: 'HOLD-for-operator-review is non-negotiable at launch; auto-route to curmudgeon is a future relaxation after track record'). A parallel curmudgeon preview would (a) consume curmudgeon Opus tokens on prose the operator may reject, (b) create a race condition (curmudgeon flags an issue while operator is reviewing), (c) confuse the EXP lifecycle (which signal — operator-approve or curmudgeon-issue — takes precedence?). Cleaner to gate strictly on operator-approve, then auto-push to curmudgeon AFTER integration (same as any normal EXP integration — curmudgeon reviews the integrated wins.json/sections.json prose).
D_Q1_HOLD_queue_vs_parallel_preview.decider_intake_extension_summary: When decider Step 2a reads an EXP file with `mode === 'compression'` AND `hold_for_operator_review === true`: (1) skip the integration write, (2) add an entry to monitor/decisions/compression-hold-queue.json (new file) with EXP id, target, ratio, authored_at, operator_review_required_flag, (3) write a one-line note to latest-decider-summary.txt pointing the operator at the EXP file path + side-by-side comparison HTML path (analogous to EXP-401's comparison HTML at root of repo), (4) leave the tracker entry status='complete' but add `awaiting_operator_review: true` field, (5) on a subsequent decider run, check monitor/analyst/human-notes.json for an operator-approval HNOTE referencing the compression EXP id; if present, proceed with integration.

operator_approval_HNOTE_shape.approval_notes: free-form operator notes (e.g., 'much better', 'cut more from the third paragraph', 'follow-up ISS is right, file it')

D_Q2_integration_path_and_additive_field.answer: On operator-approve, decider integrates via the standard wins.json/sections.json edit + test.js gate + commit pattern (identical to EXP-401 commit 66c9a74 mechanics). The EXP file gets additive lifecycle fields appended.

additive_lifecycle_fields_on_EXP.status: transitions 'pending-operator-review' → 'integrated' (one-way per the DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception pattern in CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules — extend the exception to compression EXPs)
additive_lifecycle_fields_on_EXP.integrated_at: ISO timestamp set at decider integration time
additive_lifecycle_fields_on_EXP.integration_commit_sha: git SHA of the integration commit
additive_lifecycle_fields_on_EXP.operator_approval_hnote_id: the HNOTE id that signaled approval
additive_lifecycle_fields_on_EXP.follow_up_iss_filed: ISS-NNN if operator chose to file the follow-up; null if skipped

tracker_entry_additive_fields.integration_mode: 'compression_operator_approved' (new value extending the existing integration_mode enum that includes 'no_op_confirmation', 'insert_after', etc.)

D_Q2_integration_path_and_additive_field.post_integration_queue_push: After integration, decider pushes the WIN to the curmudgeon priority queue with class='verification' (compression is a verbosity-reduction edit, not a new-argument edit — verification class is appropriate). reason: 'Compression EXP-NNN integrated — verify compressed prose preserves verdict, claim, and load-bearing numbers per EXP-NNN.content_preservation_audit'. context_hints.source_file: 'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN.json'. Curmudgeon's post-integration review serves as the second-eye on the operator's approval — catches preservation-audit drift that operator may have missed.
D_Q2_integration_path_and_additive_field.test_js_gate: MUST pass before push. Same as any decider integration (existing decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md Step 5 test-gate language applies).

D_Q3_follow_up_iss_auto_file_vs_operator.answer: SURFACE TO OPERATOR for explicit approval. Decider does NOT auto-file the follow-up ISS even if the compression EXP includes follow_up_issue_proposed.
D_Q3_follow_up_iss_auto_file_vs_operator.rationale: Compression cuts that propose follow-up ISS (per EXP-401's GRACE-forensics example) are editorial-judgment recommendations from the analyst — the cut content might be (a) a structurally-separate argument that genuinely deserves its own surface (file the ISS), or (b) content the operator decides is fine to drop entirely (don't file the ISS), or (c) content that should be merged into another WIN instead of getting its own surface. Auto-filing without operator weigh-in risks ISS-pollution. Surface-to-operator preserves the editorial decision in operator's hands. Operator signals via the approval HNOTE's `follow_up_iss_decision: 'file' | 'skip'` field.
D_Q3_follow_up_iss_auto_file_vs_operator.mechanism: Compression EXP includes follow_up_issue_proposed object (per EXP-401 pattern). Decider HOLD-queue entry surfaces this in latest-decider-summary.txt: 'EXP-NNN proposes follow-up ISS: <suggested_iss_title>. Operator approval HNOTE should include follow_up_iss_decision: file|skip.' On operator-approve with `follow_up_iss_decision: 'file'`, decider creates ISS-NNN in open-issues.json with severity from the proposal (default 'moderate'), source='compression-EXP-NNN-followup'. On `follow_up_iss_decision: 'skip'`, decider records the skip in the EXP's lifecycle fields (`follow_up_iss_filed: null, follow_up_iss_skipped_by_operator: true`) and proceeds with integration.

D_Q4_anchor_format_normalization.answer: BOTH — decider normalizes known anchor patterns at integration time AND test.js stays as the backstop. Document the normalization rule in decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md Step 2a's compression branch.
D_Q4_anchor_format_normalization.rationale: EXP-401's anchor bug (`#part6.html#p6-614-kappa-cluster` in EXP draft → test.js rejected → operator manually corrected to `#p6-614-kappa-cluster` at integration commit 66c9a74) is a recurring failure mode if compression EXPs are authored by an LLM analyst that doesn't have the rendered anchor table in context. Test.js catching it is the safety net, but failing the gate means the EXP needs a re-write loop (analyst run → EXP authored → decider attempts integration → test fails → analyst re-runs → ...). A normalization pass at decider integration time removes the loop for the known pattern (`#partN.html#anchor` → `#anchor`) and lets test.js catch genuinely-novel anchor bugs.
D_Q4_anchor_format_normalization.normalization_rule: Before test.js gate, decider scans the proposed_html for the pattern `href="#part\d+\.html#([^"]+)"` (or similar) and rewrites to `href="#$1"`. Logged in the integration commit message as 'Anchor format normalized: <count> instances of #partN.html#X → #X'. If test.js still fails after normalization, escalate to operator via stranded-patches mechanism (existing).
D_Q4_anchor_format_normalization.scope_caveat: Normalization applies ONLY to compression EXPs at this PROP's launch. If the bug recurs for non-compression EXPs (e.g., Mode 1 deep-attack EXPs), file a separate amendment to extend the normalization to all decider integrations. The conservative scoping prevents accidental rewrite of intentional partN.html anchors elsewhere.
D_Q4_anchor_format_normalization.test_js_backstop: Test.js continues to enforce the anchor-format rule as today. The normalization is a pre-test pass that handles the known failure mode; test.js handles unknown failures.

Q1_dispatcher_placement_option_recommend.answer: Option C — reuse PROP-043's Mode 0.5 commission infrastructure. Compression-mode is one commission_type plugging into the existing dispatcher tier. No new Mode 6.
Q1_dispatcher_placement_option_recommend.rationale_one_paragraph: PROP-043 (filed and authored at 2026-05-16T19-30 per its directive completion) provides Mode 0.5 as a clean priority-routing tier above Mode 1. PROP-043's Mode 0.5 dispatcher block already says: 'If commission_type is `'compression'`, see PROP-042 procedure (TBD — landing in a separate amendment).' PROP-042 IS that procedure. Creating a parallel Mode 6 would duplicate Mode 0.5's priority infrastructure, force operator to decide 'which Mode picks this up' for every new commission type, and fragment the operator-commission surface in the prompt. Option B (generalize Mode 5 in place) was rejected in PROP-043 for the same reasons it's rejected here: Mode 5 has a specific named contract ('Frozen Prediction Drafts' with OTS-anchored immutable JSON deliverable), and bundling compression under it overloads the contract. Option A (new Mode 6) would create the same overloading problem in reverse — compression isn't a 'priority tier', it's a 'work shape' that the priority tier (Mode 0.5) routes to. Option C is the clean separation: PROP-043 owns the routing, PROP-042 owns the work-shape spec.

pros: Dedicated Mode for compression — easy to find in the dispatcher
Parallel symmetry with Mode 5 (each operator-commission type gets its own Mode)
cons: Duplicates PROP-043's Mode 0.5 priority-routing logic — every commission type needs its own Mode-N if this pattern continues
Mode 5 vs Mode 6 priority ordering becomes a Q (FIFO? Mode 5 first because pre-existing?)
Operator has to remember which Mode handles which commission type at HNOTE-filing time
Counter to PROP-043's design intent (which explicitly anticipated PROP-042 as commission_type='compression' delegation)

pros: Single 'operator commissions' surface in the prompt
cons: Overloads Mode 5's named contract: 'Frozen Prediction Drafts' is a SPECIFIC deliverable (OTS-anchored JSON). Bundling compression dilutes the meaning
Same issues PROP-043 rejected for Option C — see PROP-043 selected_option rationale
Doesn't compose with PROP-043's Mode 0.5 (would create two routing surfaces)

pros: Reuses PROP-043's existing dispatcher infrastructure — zero new dispatcher complexity
PROP-043 explicitly anticipated this delegation (says 'TBD — landing in a separate amendment')
PROP-042 stays tightly scoped: HNOTE-schema + EXP-shape + decider-intake; no analyst-dispatcher changes
Forward-compatible: future commission types (custom-rewrite, ...) plug in the same way
cons: Dependency on PROP-043 landing — if PROP-043 stalls in operator review, PROP-042 can't ship
Mitigation: ship together (Q5 below) or sequence PROP-043 first within a coordinated review session

Q2_Option_A_priority_relative_to_Mode_5.answer: N/A — Option C selected. PROP-043 Mode 0.5 handles all commission types FIFO by created_at within the same tier. No Mode 5 vs Mode 6 priority question.
Q2_Option_A_priority_relative_to_Mode_5.if_option_A_were_selected_hypothetical: FIFO across commission types (created_at ascending). 'Mode 5 first because pre-existing' is a process artifact, not a design principle; the operator's editorial intent should set the order, and FIFO reflects that. 'Mode 6 first because compression is operator's near-term focus' is a temporary state — if it becomes permanent, the operator should re-prioritize Mode 5 manually by re-filing pending frozen-pred HNOTEs after compression ones.

Q3_Option_B_migration_plan.answer: N/A — Option C selected. No Mode 5 migration needed.
Q3_Option_B_migration_plan.if_option_B_were_selected_hypothetical: Mode 5's body-detection regex would have to widen to `/frozen[- ]prediction|compression|<future-types>/i.test(...)`. Backward-compat: existing 'frozen prediction' HNOTEs continue to match. New 'compression' HNOTEs match via the new regex term. New compression-specific procedure section under Mode 5 would route by body-detected commission_type. This is exactly the bundling that overloads Mode 5's contract — which is why Option B is rejected.

Q4_Option_C_implications_and_simpler_PRO.answer: Confirmed. Option C makes PROP-042 a tightly-scoped HNOTE-schema + EXP-shape + decider-intake spec. No analyst-dispatcher prompt changes (PROP-043 owns the dispatcher). No new Mode in the dispatcher count. Simpler review surface for the operator.

Q5_sequencing_with_PROP_043_dispatcher_f.answer: PROP-043 lands FIRST, then PROP-042. Author together; review together; sequence the apply phases (PROP-043 first, PROP-042 second).
Q5_sequencing_with_PROP_043_dispatcher_f.rationale: PROP-043 provides the Mode 0.5 dispatcher tier that PROP-042 plugs into. If PROP-042 ships first, its delegation target (Mode 0.5) doesn't exist — analyst doesn't pick up compression HNOTEs. If PROP-043 ships without PROP-042, Mode 0.5 has a 'compression → TBD' delegation that returns a 'no procedure defined' fallthrough — usable but incomplete. The strict ordering: (1) operator reviews and approves both PROPs in one session; (2) cowork-claude + operator apply PROP-043 first (adds Mode 0.5 to analyst.md, CLAUDE.md discipline note, optional tinker telemetry); (3) cowork-claude + operator apply PROP-042 (adds compression procedure under Mode 0.5, EXP schema docs, decider intake extension, CLAUDE.md discipline note); (4) optionally test with a synthetic compression HNOTE before retiring the operator-direct escape hatch.
Q5_sequencing_with_PROP_043_dispatcher_f.coordination_with_PROP_043: PROP-043's headline already says 'PROP-043 first, then PROP-042' (per its open_questions_for_operator Q-OP-A043-4). PROP-042 confirms the same ordering from its own perspective. No coordination conflict.
Q5_sequencing_with_PROP_043_dispatcher_f.alternative_considered: Could ship PROP-042 first IF Mode 0.5's behavior were temporarily inlined into PROP-042's analyst.md block (a localized routing check for commission HNOTEs in the analyst dispatcher, with the same logic Mode 0.5 will eventually own). Rejected — duplicates the routing logic, then has to be removed when PROP-043 lands, causing avoidable churn. Cleaner to sequence strictly.

files_to_change.verbatim_block_to_insert:
**EXP file schema for mode='compression' (PROP-042):** compression EXPs use a specialized shape that signals decider to HOLD for operator review instead of auto-routing to curmudgeon. Required fields:

- `mode`: 'compression' (literal string)
- `authored_by`: 'analyst' or 'cowork-claude (operator-direct, in-session)' or similar
- `authored_at`: ISO timestamp
- `source`: HNOTE-ID that commissioned this EXP
- `target_win_id` OR `target_section_id`: the surface being compressed (exactly one)
- `target_field`: which field on the target (e.g., 'detail_evidence', 'section_body')
- `current_word_count`: integer — pre-compression word count of the target_field text
- `proposed_word_count`: integer — post-compression word count of proposed_html
- `target_word_count`: integer — operator's target from the commission HNOTE
- `compression_ratio`: string (e.g., '3.58') — current/proposed ratio
- `proposed_html`: string — the compressed prose, HTML with hyperlinks preserved
- `rationale`: string — analyst's prose explaining cut decisions and preservation
- `cuts_summary`: array of {cut: string, approx_words: integer, destination: string} — one entry per substantive cut, destination explains where the content went
- `content_preservation_audit`: object with subfields:
  - `verdict_unchanged`: boolean
  - `verdict`: string — the verdict that was preserved (or null if not verdict-bearing)
  - `claim_unchanged`: boolean
  - `claim`: string — the claim text preserved (or null)
  - `numbers_preserved`: array of strings (numbers + units)
  - `numbers_dropped`: array of strings — with destination annotation
  - `citations_preserved`: array of strings
  - `citations_dropped`: array of strings — with destination annotation
  - `cross_win_refs_preserved`: array of WIN-NNN ids
  - `structural_section_ref_preserved`: string or null
  - `argument_structure_summary`: string — high-level summary of what argument shape was preserved
- `follow_up_issue_proposed`: object or null — {proposal, suggested_iss_title, suggested_iss_severity} when cut content deserves its own surface; operator decides file-vs-skip
- `hold_for_operator_review`: true (boolean, always)
- `do_not_route_to_curmudgeon_until_operator_approves`: true (boolean, always)
- `status`: 'pending-operator-review' at authoring; transitions to 'integrated' (or 'rejected'/'revising') after operator decision
- Additive lifecycle fields appended at integration time (decider-owned, additive-edit exception): `integrated_at`, `integrated_by`, `integration_commit_sha`, `operator_approval_hnote_id`, `follow_up_iss_filed` (or null), `follow_up_iss_skipped_by_operator` (bool)

**review_class field semantics for compression EXPs:** Compression EXPs do NOT set `review_class` at author time — they bypass the standard analyst-to-curmudgeon routing. After operator-approved integration, decider pushes the WIN to curmudgeon priority queue with class='verification' (compression is verbosity-reduction, not new-argument). See `decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md` Step 2a compression branch for the post-integration queue push.

**Worked example:** see `monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-401-WIN-012-compression-experiment.json` — the operator-direct compression that established this shape (WIN-012.detail_evidence 1888w → 528w, 3.58x, integrated commit 66c9a74).

**File ownership:** compression EXPs are append-only at authoring (analyst writes). Decider's integration-time additive-edit fields are a PROP-042 extension to the DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception pattern (CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules). The additive fields above replace no existing fields and are idempotent (guarded by `if (exp.<field>==null)`). The only mutated field is `status`, allowed exactly one one-way transition: 'pending-operator-review' → 'integrated' (or 'rejected' or 'revising' — both one-way to terminal states).

2.5. **CHECK FOR `mode === 'compression'` BEFORE STEP 3.** If the expansion output file has `mode === 'compression'` AND `hold_for_operator_review === true` AND `do_not_route_to_curmudgeon_until_operator_approves === true`, this is a PROP-042 compression EXP. Route via HOLD-for-operator-review:

   a. Do NOT write a patch (the proposed_html requires operator side-by-side review first).
   b. Do NOT push to the curmudgeon priority queue.
   c. Do NOT close issues (the EXP is not yet integrated; no issues are resolved by the EXP existence).
   d. Read the EXP fields and validate compression-specific schema: mode, target_win_id-or-target_section_id, target_field, content_preservation_audit (all required subfields present). If validation fails, log 'EXP-NNN compression schema malformed: <missing fields>' to daily report and leave the tracker entry status='complete' but DO NOT mark it integrated. Operator will see the malformed flag in latest-decider-summary.txt and either fix the EXP or revise the source HNOTE.
   e. **Anchor normalization pass (PROP-042 D-Q4):** scan EXP.proposed_html for the pattern `href="#part\d+\.html#([^"]+)"` and rewrite to `href="#$1"`. Log count of normalized anchors in the integration prep notes (`compression_hold_prep_log.normalized_anchor_count`).
   f. Add an entry to monitor/decisions/compression-hold-queue.json (create file if absent — append-only):
      ```json
      {
        "exp_id": "EXP-NNN",
        "target_win_id": "<from EXP>",
        "target_field": "<from EXP>",
        "current_word_count": <from EXP>,
        "proposed_word_count": <from EXP>,
        "compression_ratio": "<from EXP>",
        "authored_at": "<EXP.authored_at>",
        "hold_added_at": "<now>",
        "hold_added_by_run": "<decider RUN_ID>",
        "comparison_html_path": "<if a side-by-side HTML exists at repo root, link it; else null>",
        "operator_review_required": true,
        "follow_up_issue_proposed": <from EXP — copy verbatim or null>,
        "normalized_anchor_count": <from prep log>,
        "approval_hnote_expected_at": "monitor/analyst/human-notes.json (id matching HNOTE-OPERATOR-APPROVE-COMPRESSION-EXP-NNN)"
      }
      ```
   g. Write a one-line entry to latest-decider-summary.txt under a 'Compression EXPs awaiting operator review' section: 'EXP-NNN: WIN-NNN.<field> <current_word_count>w → <proposed_word_count>w (<ratio>x). Review: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN.json. Approve via HNOTE-OPERATOR-APPROVE-COMPRESSION-EXP-NNN.'
   h. Mark the tracker entry status='complete' (unchanged) but add `awaiting_operator_review: true` and `compression_hold_added_at: <now>` fields.
   i. Move to the next expansion. STOP compression EXP processing for this run.

2.6. **CHECK FOR PENDING COMPRESSION APPROVAL HNOTES.** Each decider run, after Step 2 no_op handling, scan monitor/analyst/human-notes.json for notes with `target_agents: ['decider']` AND `id` matching `HNOTE-OPERATOR-APPROVE-COMPRESSION-EXP-*` AND `status: 'pending'`. For each matching approval HNOTE:

   a. Read the referenced compression EXP file. Validate it's status='pending-operator-review' with mode='compression'.
   b. Read approval_decision:
      - `'approve'`: proceed to integration (steps c-h below).
      - `'reject'`: mark EXP status='rejected'. Mark tracker entry status='rejected-by-operator' with `rejected_at`, `rejected_by_run`. Write a one-line entry to the daily report. Mark approval HNOTE consumed. DO NOT integrate.
      - `'revise'`: mark EXP status='revising'. Write an analyst-side HNOTE (`monitor/analyst/human-notes.json`) commissioning the revision (operator's `approval_notes` becomes the revision body). Mark approval HNOTE consumed. DO NOT integrate.
   c. (approve path) Read the (anchor-normalized) proposed_html from the EXP file. Apply to data/wins.json[id=<target_win_id>].<target_field> (or data/sections.json equivalent).
   d. Run `node test.js` (or the build's test command). MUST PASS. If fails, escalate via stranded-patches mechanism with `stranded_reason: 'compression_integration_test_fail'` and leave EXP status='pending-operator-review' (operator decides whether to revise EXP or amend HNOTE).
   e. Regenerate docs/index.html via `node build.js html`.
   f. Run `node test.js` again (post-build). MUST PASS.
   g. Commit with message: `compression integration: <target_win_id>.<target_field> operator-approved compression (<current>w → <proposed>w, <ratio>x) — EXP-NNN`. Push.
   h. Apply EXP additive lifecycle fields: status='integrated', integrated_at, integrated_by, integration_commit_sha, operator_approval_hnote_id. Apply tracker entry additive fields: integrated=true, integrated_at, integration_mode='compression_operator_approved'.
   i. Process follow_up_issue_proposed per the approval HNOTE's `follow_up_iss_decision`:
      - `'file'`: create new ISS in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json with severity from EXP.follow_up_issue_proposed.suggested_iss_severity (default 'moderate'), description from EXP.follow_up_issue_proposed.proposal, source='compression-EXP-NNN-followup'. Apply EXP field `follow_up_iss_filed: <new-ISS-id>`.
      - `'skip'`: apply EXP field `follow_up_iss_filed: null, follow_up_iss_skipped_by_operator: true`.
      - Field absent and follow_up_issue_proposed is null on EXP: no action.
   j. Push the WIN to curmudgeon priority queue (post-integration): class='verification', reason: 'Compression EXP-NNN integrated — verify compressed prose preserves verdict, claim, and load-bearing numbers per EXP-NNN.content_preservation_audit', context_hints.source_file: 'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN.json'.
   k. Mark approval HNOTE consumed via the standard mechanism. Daily report: 'Compression EXP-NNN integrated: <target> <ratio>x, follow-up ISS <filed/skipped>, curmudgeon queue push at queue_id=N'.

files_to_change.verbatim_text_to_insert: **Compression-commission discipline (PROP-042, 2026-05-17):** Compression HNOTEs (`commission: true`, `commission_type: 'compression'`) are OPERATOR-COMMISSIONED ONLY — analyst does NOT self-trigger compression. The commission channel of record is `monitor/analyst/human-notes.json` (analyst-exclusive — see C-Q4 in PROP-042); curmudgeon-side and decider-side HNOTEs are not channels for compression. Post-integration, the WIN goes to curmudgeon priority queue with class='verification' for preservation-audit second-eye. Discipline cadence: compression commissions should match the over-target backlog drain rate; operator-direct in-session compression (the EXP-401 escape hatch) remains available for cases where Mode 0.5 wait time is unacceptable.

PROP-043's Mode 0.5 picks it up in the analyst's next run (FIFO within Mode 0.5). Decider's next run sees the EXP, recognizes mode='compression' + hold_for_operator_review=true, normalizes anchors, writes to compression-hold-queue.json, surfaces to operator in latest-decider-summary.txt. Operator reviews EXP file + (optional) side-by-side comparison HTML. Operator files HNOTE-OPERATOR-APPROVE-COMPRESSION-EXP-NNN with approval_decision='approve'|'reject'|'revise'. Decider's next run consumes the approval HNOTE: on approve, integrate to wins.json/sections.json, run test.js, build, run test.js, commit, push, push to curmudgeon priority queue with class='verification' for preservation audit second-eye, mark all lifecycle fields. Curmudgeon reviews the integrated prose as a normal verification-class queue item.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: EXP-401 worked because cowork-claude did it directly in-session. Just keep doing that — formalize nothing, accept that operator commissions are operator-direct.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Scales poorly. The Phase 2 rewriter's first run (2026-05-16) produced 1 RW + 4 PUNTs — 3 of the 4 PUNTs were length-dominated WINs the rewriter correctly deferred. If those 3 (and the existing ~30 over-target WIN backlog) each need a cowork-claude session, we're bottlenecked on operator availability AND operator attention-budget for compression work. The infrastructure cost of PROP-042 is modest (~120 lines across 4 files, mostly in reference docs); the operator-direct cost scales linearly with backlog size. Mode 0.5 + compression-mode lets operator file-and-walk-away while still gating on operator review at integration. The operator-direct escape hatch (running cowork-claude in-session) remains available for genuinely-can't-wait cases.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Strong rebuttal — backlog drain rate decisively favors formalization.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: Compression risks scientific-precision drift more than mechanical rewriting does. EXP-401 was reviewed by operator before integration, but at scale that's a lot of operator review. The fix is to reduce the need for compression (better WIN-authoring discipline upfront).
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Better-upfront-authoring is the right long-term play and SHOULD be encouraged — but the existing 30+ over-target WIN surfaces exist NOW and need handling. Discipline applies forward; compression cleans up the backlog. The drift risk is real and is precisely why HOLD-for-operator-review is non-negotiable at launch (and why the post-integration curmudgeon class='verification' push provides a second-eye on preservation). The operator-review burden is bounded by the commission rate, which the operator controls via filing discipline (per PROP-043 commission flag discipline). If operator-review burden becomes unsustainable (e.g., >2-3 compression reviews per week consistently), that's a signal to either (a) tighten the upfront authoring rules, (b) introduce a triage gate (analyst pre-filters which compression EXPs are 'low-risk' vs 'high-risk' for operator's review queue), or (c) revisit whether some compressions can proceed without the HOLD gate. None of those amendments require PROP-042 changes — they're future amendments.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Strong counter — drift risk is real; the design's HOLD gate is the load-bearing mitigation.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: Compression-mode as analyst commission overlaps with what the operator can do directly with cowork-claude (as demonstrated by EXP-401). Building infrastructure for a workflow operator can do ad-hoc creates maintenance overhead for negligible automation benefit.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Depends on steady-state compression volume. If 1-2 compressions/month, operator-direct is fine and PROP-042 is over-engineered. If 5+/week, automation pays off significantly. Current observable evidence: ~30 over-target WIN surfaces (per CLAUDE.md cited backlog) + new ones surfaced by Phase 2 rewriter's PUNT pattern. Even at a conservative 1 compression/week post-launch, that's 30+ weeks of operator-direct work vs ~30 file-and-walk-away analyst-runs after PROP-042. The maintenance overhead of PROP-042 is bounded (the spec is mostly schema definitions; the procedure is short; the lifecycle mirrors existing EXP patterns). Telemetry recommendation: track pending_compression_hold_count and oldest_compression_hold_days for 30 days post-launch. If volume stays at <1/month with no HOLD-queue buildup, consider reverting PROP-042 to operator-direct only. If volume hits the expected 1-3/week range, the automation justifies itself.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Moderate rebuttal — quantified per-week threshold helps the operator decide. Telemetry-driven 30-day re-evaluation is the right discipline.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: The compression work is editorial judgment; an LLM analyst will fail at it. Even cowork-claude only succeeded with operator-attended interactive review. A 'fire and forget' pipeline analyst will produce poor compressions that waste operator review time.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Two parts. (1) EXP-401 was authored by cowork-claude WITHOUT interactive review during authoring — operator reviewed AFTER the EXP was complete (the in-session-ness gave operator faster turnaround on the review, but didn't change the authoring shape). The PROP-042 procedure makes the analyst's compression work follow the same shape: read HNOTE preservation list → draft → fill content_preservation_audit → emit EXP. Operator reviews the EXP file (same surface). The only difference is who runs the model (cowork-claude in-session vs pipeline analyst on schedule). (2) Quality risk is bounded by HOLD-for-operator-review. If pipeline analyst's compressions are systematically worse than cowork-claude's, operator reject rate rises — visible signal. If reject rate >50% for first 5 EXPs, PROP-042 needs amendment (e.g., switching to higher-capacity model for compression work, or tightening the HNOTE preservation list requirements). The cost of detecting this is bounded (5 EXPs × ~1 analyst-run each = 5 hours of operator-attention spread over a week).
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Moderate counter — quality risk is real but observable and bounded.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: PROP-042 depends on PROP-043. If PROP-043 stalls in operator review, PROP-042 ships nothing. Two-PROP dependencies are fragile.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Acknowledged. Mitigation: ship together in a coordinated review session per Q5. PROP-043's headline_recommendation says the same thing (Q-OP-A043-4: 'PROP-043 first, then PROP-042'). The two PROPs were authored sequentially with PROP-042 explicitly designed to plug into PROP-043's Mode 0.5 — they're complementary, not competing. If operator rejects PROP-043 entirely (e.g., decides Mode 0.5 is wrong abstraction), PROP-042 would need amendment to define its own analyst-dispatcher hook (functionally equivalent to PROP-043's Option B 'top-of-dispatcher check' but scoped to compression). That amendment is straightforward (~10 lines of analyst.md) but adds prompt-complexity; the cleaner path remains the coordinated PROP-043+PROP-042 ship.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Moderate counter — addressed by sequencing discipline.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: The HOLD queue introduces a new persistent state in monitor/decisions/compression-hold-queue.json. New state means new failure modes (corruption, lost entries, race conditions with workspace-sync).
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: True but bounded. The HOLD queue is append-only at the entry level (entries added; status flipped one-way; no bulk rewrites). The classification is 'git-owned with decider writes from clone' — same pattern as monitor/decisions/closure-ledger.jsonl which has run for months without corruption. Workspace-sync NEVER_PUSH excludes it (decider writes from clone, workspace-sync doesn't touch). Race conditions limited to: decider writes entry, operator files approval HNOTE before workspace-sync rescues; decider's next run sees the approval HNOTE and the entry simultaneously — no race. If the file is lost or corrupted, the EXP file (`monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN.json`) is the authoritative source — the HOLD queue is just a routing convenience. Worst case: decider rebuilds the HOLD queue by scanning expansions/ for mode='compression' status='pending-operator-review' EXPs. Recovery procedure is straightforward.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Weak counter — failure modes are graceful and recoverable.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: Analyst-exclusive scoping (C-Q4) is overly restrictive. Curmudgeon may legitimately want to commission compression when it discovers a verbosity-induced review pathology.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Acknowledged that curmudgeon may surface the NEED for compression. The right channel for that is an ISS routed to operator-review-suggest, NOT a curmudgeon-side compression HNOTE. The discipline preserves a single channel of record (monitor/analyst/human-notes.json) for compression commissions, which (a) simplifies operator's mental model (one place to file), (b) keeps the curmudgeon role focused on review (not commission), (c) prevents future drift where 'compression' becomes a curmudgeon-side decision the operator didn't sign off on. The cost is one extra hop (curmudgeon flags issue → operator decides → operator files compression HNOTE) but that's the right discipline for editorial decisions.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Weak-to-moderate counter — channel-of-record discipline outweighs the convenience of cross-agent commissions.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: One-surface-per-HNOTE (C-Q3) doesn't scale when the operator wants to compress 5+ surfaces in one editorial sitting. Five HNOTEs is friction.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Per C-Q3 future amendment path: if operator hits this friction in practice (>3 compression HNOTEs filed in one sitting), PROP-042-amendment-001 can add parent-batch HNOTE schema. Deferring is the right call because (a) we don't yet know what 'batch' means in practice (5 unrelated WINs? 5 WINs in one section cluster? 5 fields on one WIN?), (b) Mode 0.5 FIFO processes them at the same throughput either way, (c) the operator-review burden of N compressions is the same whether filed as 1 batch or N HNOTEs. Friction at filing time (5 HNOTEs vs 1 batch) is real but bounded — operator can script the multi-HNOTE-file with a small node script. Deferring batch-schema until operator has filed >3 in one sitting at least once gives us the use case to design against.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Moderate counter — future-amendable, current scope holds.

operator_other_side_considerations._note_per_operator_preference: Operator's stated preference: 'Think about the other side of the argument too.' This section steelmans the case for NOT building PROP-042 at all, beyond the per-counter rebuttals above. Tinker's net recommendation is to ship PROP-042 (per the headline_recommendation), but the operator should weigh these meta-considerations before approving.

case_against_PROP_042.argument: Compression is a one-time backlog cleanup, not a recurring need.
case_against_PROP_042.elaboration: If the ~30 over-target WIN surfaces are a one-time accumulation (and forward authoring discipline prevents new accumulation), PROP-042's infrastructure is amortized over a single 30-EXP burn rather than ongoing throughput. Operator-direct compression of 30 EXPs over 6-12 months (1-3/week) is workable WITHOUT formalization — the existing cowork-claude pattern handles it. PROP-042's marginal benefit comes from steady-state compression demand; if steady-state is near zero, the infrastructure is over-built for the use case. Counter-counter: even one-time backlog clearing benefits from file-and-walk-away (operator doesn't have to sit through 30 cowork-claude sessions). Net: weak support for shipping vs deferring.

case_against_PROP_042.argument: The PROP-041 rewriter's PUNT pattern suggests the right fix is in the rewriter, not the analyst.
case_against_PROP_042.elaboration: PROP-041 Phase 2 produced 4 PUNTs on length-dominated WINs because Categories A-H can't reduce content. The instinctive fix is to add Category I (content compression) to the rewriter. But the directive's out_of_scope_for_tinker[1] explicitly forbids 'changes to the rewriter (sloppytoppy-rewrite.md) — compression-mode is analyst infrastructure, not rewriter infrastructure'. The directive's reasoning (per DIRECTIVE-005 addendum): rewriter is mechanical/restructure-only; compression is editorial-judgment work. Counter to that reasoning: maybe the rewriter SHOULD handle low-risk compression (e.g., 'this paragraph repeats the prior paragraph's claim, drop it'), with editorial-judgment compression reserved for analyst. Splitting the work between rewriter (mechanical compression) and analyst (editorial compression) might better fit the workload. Counter-counter: this splits the design surface and creates two compression paths — added complexity. The directive's strict scoping (analyst-only) is defensible but the alternative (rewriter+analyst split) is also defensible and could be PROP-042-amendment-001 if operator-direct experience suggests the split is worth it. Net: moderate support for the directive's scoping with a clear amendment path if evidence warrants.

case_against_PROP_042.argument: Pipeline analyst quality risk is hard to bound a priori.
case_against_PROP_042.elaboration: EXP-401 was authored by cowork-claude (Opus 4.7 with operator-attended workflow context). Pipeline analyst is the same model family but runs unattended. Initial quality unknown — could be similar to EXP-401, could be significantly worse if the pipeline analyst lacks the 'review what you wrote' loop that cowork-claude naturally has. If quality is poor, operator reject rate climbs, operator-review burden grows, and the infrastructure cost isn't recovered. Counter-counter: HOLD-for-operator-review is the safety net. Even at 50% reject rate, the operator's only cost is the review time (no integration of bad compressions). Mitigation: stage PROP-042 with a 'first 5 EXPs' calibration period — if reject rate > 50%, freeze new compression HNOTEs and reassess. Net: real risk; the HOLD gate is sufficient mitigation; explicit calibration period is a discipline-add tinker recommends.

PROP-043 stalls in operator review → PROP-042 can't ship. Or PROP-043 ships but the Mode 0.5 abstraction proves wrong and gets reverted → PROP-042's delegation target disappears. Counter-counter: tinker's net assessment is that PROP-043 is well-designed and likely to ship (it has 7 counter-arguments addressed, comprehensive Q-OP answers, simple scope). The dependency risk is real but low-probability. The amendment path (define analyst-dispatcher hook inside PROP-042 if PROP-043 reverts) is straightforward. Net: real risk; coordinated review session mitigates.

case_against_PROP_042.argument: The HOLD queue mechanism is novel and untested. Existing decider integration paths are well-worn.
case_against_PROP_042.elaboration: Decider's existing integration paths (EXP integration in Step 2a, M2 auto-close at Step 8b, M3 carry-over at Step 8c) have been refined over months and tested in production. PROP-042 introduces a NEW path (HOLD-for-operator-review + operator-approval-HNOTE consumption) that doesn't exist elsewhere. New paths have unknown failure modes. Counter-counter: the new path is structurally similar to existing patterns (additive-edit on EXP, queue entry in a JSONL-like file, scan-for-approval-HNOTE at each run). The mechanism is novel but the building blocks are well-worn. Comprehensive test patterns (test_1..test_11) cover the new failure modes. Net: real novelty risk; test coverage and gradual rollout (first 5 EXPs as calibration) are the mitigations.

operator_other_side_considerations.tinker_net_judgment: PROP-042 should ship. The motivating need is real (30+ over-target WINs + new ones surfaced by Phase 2 rewriter PUNTs), the EXP-401 worked-example demonstrates viability (operator confirmed 'much better'), the design carefully scopes to analyst-exclusive operator-commissioned work, HOLD-for-operator-review preserves the editorial-judgment gate, and PROP-043 dependency is mitigable via coordinated review. The strongest counter (compression risks scientific drift) is the design's load-bearing constraint and is addressed by both the HOLD gate AND the post-integration curmudgeon class='verification' review. Tinker recommends shipping with two discipline-adds: (1) explicit 30-day post-launch telemetry review (per counter 3), (2) first-5-EXPs calibration with freeze-and-reassess if reject rate >50% (per counter 4 / case-against #3). Both are operator-side disciplines, not PROP-042 scope.

phase_2_followups_not_in_scope: Multi-surface batch compression HNOTE schema (per C-Q3 future amendment path). Defer until operator hits >3 single-sitting HNOTEs.
Cross-agent compression channels (curmudgeon-side or decider-side commissions). Defer per C-Q4 — current channel-of-record discipline holds.

Auto-route compression EXPs to curmudgeon parallel-with-operator-review (per D-Q1 — currently HOLD-only). Future relaxation contingent on first-N-EXPs track record showing curmudgeon catches preservation drift that operator misses.

Rewriter-side compression category (Category I or split between mechanical-compression and editorial-compression). Per directive's out_of_scope, but tinker flags this as a candidate PROP-042-amendment-001 if operator-direct experience suggests the split.
Decider auto-file follow-up ISS for compression cuts (per D-Q3 — currently operator-approves explicitly). Future relaxation contingent on follow-up ISS proposals having a stable signal.
Anchor normalization expansion to all decider integrations (per D-Q4 — currently compression-only). Defer until evidence the bug recurs for non-compression EXPs.
Tinker telemetry for compression HOLD queue depth (per files_to_change[4] — optional at launch). Add when volume warrants.

Generic 'commission-mode router' that delegates from Mode 0.5 to per-commission-type procedure files (analyst-mode05-compression-commission.md, analyst-mode05-frozen-pred-commission.md, etc.). Useful when 3+ commission types defined; not at PROP-042 launch.

Side-by-side comparison HTML auto-generation by analyst at EXP-write time. EXP-401 had this (EXP-401-WIN-012-compression-comparison.html at repo root, operator-authored ad-hoc). Standardizing would help operator review. Defer to PROP-042-amendment-002.

Mitigations: (a) test patterns test_3 (HOLD routing) and test_4-9 (operator-approval paths) cover the main failure modes, (b) the HOLD queue is JSONL-like append-only, (c) the EXP file remains authoritative source if HOLD queue corrupts, (d) first-5-EXP calibration period (operator-side discipline) catches systemic issues early.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Sequencing: ship PROP-042 immediately after PROP-043 (back-to-back patches), or sequence with a deliberate gap to let PROP-043 telemetry settle?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Back-to-back. PROP-043's compression_type='compression' delegation target is a known dependency; closing the dependency gap is cleaner than leaving a TBD pointer in Mode 0.5 for an extended period. Telemetry can be assessed on PROP-042's first 5 EXPs (which exercise both PROP-043 and PROP-042 paths together).

open_questions_for_operator.question: Compression-hold-queue.json: does this need a UI surface (e.g., a docs/ page listing pending HOLDs), or is the latest-decider-summary.txt entry sufficient for operator awareness?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: latest-decider-summary.txt is sufficient at launch. A UI surface (docs/compression-hold.html or a section in docs/index.html) is useful for transparency but not load-bearing. Defer to PROP-042-amendment-002 if operator wants public visibility.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Side-by-side comparison HTML: should the analyst auto-generate this at EXP-write time (like EXP-401 had), or is the JSON content_preservation_audit sufficient for operator review?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Defer to PROP-042-amendment-002. EXP-401's comparison HTML was operator-authored ad-hoc and clearly helped review (operator confirmed 'much better' on the side-by-side); standardizing would help, but it adds analyst-side complexity. First 5 EXPs without it tells us if it's needed.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Calibration period: who polices the reject-rate-threshold? Tinker's pre-flight telemetry? Operator manual check? Decider self-monitoring?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Tinker pre-flight (extend the optional compression telemetry block to include reject_rate_last_5_exps and freeze recommendation if >50%). Operator gets the signal via tinker latest-summary.txt. Decider doesn't self-monitor (out of decider's role). Add to PROP-042 if operator wants explicit; otherwise informal calibration via tinker telemetry.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Compression of section_body (vs WIN-level fields): should the spec explicitly support both at launch, or scope to WIN-level fields first and add section_body support later?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Support both at launch. EXP-401 was WIN-level (detail_evidence), but section bodies face the same backlog issue (e.g., Part 6.14 κ-cluster section is itself prose-heavy from prior EXP integrations). The schema supports `target_win_id OR target_section_id` symmetrically; the analyst procedure handles both via the same draft-and-audit pattern. Limiting to WIN-level at launch would force a future amendment when the first section_body compression HNOTE arrives.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Tracker entry creation for compression EXPs: does the analyst create a tracker entry on EXP write (so operator can see compression work-in-flight), or does the decider create one at integration time?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Analyst creates the tracker entry on EXP write with status='complete', awaiting_operator_review=true (added by decider at HOLD-queue time). This makes the work visible immediately. Decider's HOLD-queue write is the operator-facing surface; tracker entry is the agent-facing audit trail. Both serve different audiences.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Approval HNOTE auto-archiving: when operator approves and decider integrates, the approval HNOTE goes to status='consumed'. Should it then be archived to monitor/analyst/human-notes-archive.jsonl (per PROP-022 phase 2 pattern), or stay in the live file as a historical record?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Archive per PROP-022 phase 2 (live file holds only pending). Approval HNOTEs are short-lived (commission → approve → integrate → done); the EXP file's integration commit_sha + operator_approval_hnote_id field preserves the audit trail. Archive at consumption time keeps the live human-notes.json file lean.

estimated_impact.operator_facing: Compression HNOTEs flow through pipeline (analyst → decider HOLD → operator review → decider integration) without operator-direct cowork-claude session. Operator review burden per EXP is bounded by content_preservation_audit JSON read + optional side-by-side HTML (when authored). Expected review time per EXP: 5-15 minutes vs 30-60 minutes for full operator-direct cowork-claude session. Throughput gain at 1 EXP/week: ~25-45 minutes/week reclaimed.

agent_facing.analyst: +35 lines analyst.md (compression-commission sub-procedure under Mode 0.5). Per-EXP authoring cost ~equivalent to a Mode 1 deep-attack singleton (one EXP per run).
agent_facing.decider: +50 lines decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md (Step 2a compression branch + operator-approval consumption path). Per-decider-run overhead: scan for compression EXPs in tracker (negligible cost — same scan that already happens) + scan for approval HNOTEs in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json (one extra read per run). Integration runs add ~30 seconds for anchor normalization + JSON edit + test.js + build + commit + push (per EXP, only on approval-day).
agent_facing.curmudgeon: No procedure changes. Post-integration receives a verification-class queue item per compression EXP (cost: one verification-class review per integrated compression, batchable per PROP-025 if multiple integrate in the same window).
agent_facing.tinker: +18 lines tinker.md (optional compression-hold telemetry block). Per-run cost negligible (one JSON read).

estimated_impact.token_cost: Modest. Analyst per-compression-EXP authoring cost: comparable to Mode 1 deep-attack (~$0.10-0.50 input + output per EXP). At expected 1-3 EXPs/week steady state: ~$2-30/month. Decider per-run overhead: negligible (~$0.001 per run for the extra JSON scans). Tinker telemetry: ~$0.0001 per run.
estimated_impact.throughput_cost: Negligible for non-compression work. Compression-EXP days add ~1 minute to decider's runtime (anchor normalization + integration sequence). Curmudgeon's verification-class queue grows by ~1 item per integrated compression (batchable). No impact on Mode 1 deep-attack drain rate (compression commissions are operator-explicit and operator-controlled volume).

rollback_plan.if_PROP_042_misfires: Revert in three steps. (1) Delete the compression-commission sub-procedure block from analyst.md Mode 0.5; analyst stops authoring compression EXPs (in-flight compression EXPs already authored remain in expansions/ as historical records). (2) Delete the Step 2a compression branch from decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md; decider stops recognizing mode='compression' EXPs (any existing compression EXP in tracker with status='complete' awaiting_operator_review=true sits indefinitely until manually integrated or deleted). (3) Delete the CLAUDE.md compression-commission discipline note. The compression-hold-queue.json file can stay (no consumer; ignored). Test.js unchanged so no regression risk.
rollback_plan.irreversible_changes: None. PROP-042 is fully additive and reversible. Integrated compressions (those that reached integration via the operator-approval path) remain in wins.json as ordinary edits — they don't need to be reverted unless operator wants to restore pre-compression prose, which is a separate operator decision.
rollback_plan.rollback_complexity: Three-file revert (analyst.md, decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md, CLAUDE.md). Optional fourth file revert (DATA-SCHEMAS.md, tinker.md). Estimated ~15 minutes of operator time. Reversion does not require PROP-043 reversion.
rollback_plan.dependency_on_PROP_043: If PROP-043 is reverted independently, PROP-042's Mode 0.5 delegation target disappears. Analyst stops picking up compression HNOTEs (they fall to Mode 2 with normal priority). PROP-042's EXP-shape and decider intake extension continue to work (a Mode-2-authored compression EXP integrates the same way), just slower at the analyst pickup step. Not a hard dependency; just a soft throughput dependency.
